671	  the structure sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T}\), and the dual
672	  \(L \mapsto L^{-1}\) of \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible} is a
673	  two-sided tensor inverse on this subcategory.
674	\end{lemma}
675	
676	\begin{proof}
677	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial,
678	        lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible,
679	        lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,
680	        lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,
681	        lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}
682	  Direct from
683	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial,
684	        lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible} applied to
685	  \(X := C \times_S T\): the tensor product of two locally-trivial
686	  rank-one modules is locally trivial of rank one, and the dual is
687	  again locally trivial of rank one. The unit \(\mathcal{O}_{C
688	  \times_S T}\) is locally trivial of rank one (trivially), so it
689	  belongs to \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\). The
690	  associativity, unit and commutativity isomorphisms of
691	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}
692	  are isomorphisms of line bundles, hence restrict to the subtype;
693	  together with the inverse of \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}
694	  they equip the isomorphism classes of
695	  \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) with the full
696	  group-law data (associativity, unit, commutativity, inverse).
697	\end{proof}
698	
699	\begin{lemma}
700	[\(\pi_T^*\) is a tensor-functor]
701	  \label{lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj}
702	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}
703	  % NOTE: no Lean declaration yet; blocked on lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso
704	  % (which is blocked on lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal).
705	  With notation as in \cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}, the pullback
706	  functor
707	  \[
708	    \pi_T^{*} \;\;\colon\;\; \texttt{LineBundle}\,T \;\longrightarrow\;
709	                          \texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T
710	  \]
711	  of \cref{def:pullback_along_projection} is a (strong) monoidal
712	  functor: there is a canonical natural isomorphism
713	  \(\pi_T^*(\mathcal{N} \otimes_T \mathcal{N'})
714	     \xrightarrow{\sim}
715	   \pi_T^* \mathcal{N} \otimes_{C \times_S T} \pi_T^* \mathcal{N'}\)
716	  for \(\mathcal{N}, \mathcal{N'} \in \texttt{LineBundle}\,T\), and a
717	  canonical isomorphism
718	  \(\pi_T^* \mathcal{O}_T \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_{C \times_S T}\),
719	  both inherited from the analogous data on the underlying
720	  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\) functors via the
721	  pullback-tensor-compatibility of \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules.
722	\end{lemma}
723	
724	\begin{proof}
725	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
726	  On any affine open of \(T\) over which \(\mathcal{N}\) and
727	  \(\mathcal{N'}\) trivialise simultaneously, the assertion reduces to
728	  the standard fact that the extension of scalars
729	  \(B \otimes_A (M \otimes_A M')
730	   \;\cong\; (B \otimes_A M) \otimes_B (B \otimes_A M')\)
731	  for a ring map \(A \to B\) and \(A\)-modules \(M, M'\). This affine
732	  identity glues across an affine trivialising cover of \(T\) by the same
733	  flat-exactness / restriction-compatibility argument as in
734	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} (extension of scalars along \(A \to B\)
735	  is itself flat, so the comparison is an isomorphism). The compatibility
736	  with the unit, the associator, and the unitors is verified pointwise on the
737	  same cover.
738	\end{proof}
739	
740	\section{Consumer: the \(\AddCommGroup\) instance for the relative Picard sheaf}
741	\label{sec:tensorobj_consumer_acg}
742	
743	The substrate of \cref{sec:tensorobj_substrate,sec:tensorobj_onproduct_lift}
744	supplies all the data missing from \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}'s Lean
745	encoding. The consumer instance below packages the abelian-group law
746	\(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]
747	 := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{C \times_S T} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\) on the
748	relative Picard quotient via the substrate.
749	
750	\begin{theorem}
751	
752	\leanok
753	[Abelian-group instance on the relative Picard quotient via \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)]
754	  \label{thm:rel_pic_addcommgroup_via_tensorobj}
755	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp.addCommGroup_via_tensorObj}
756	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct,
757	        lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj,
758	        lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,
759	        lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,
760	        lem:tensorobj_comm_iso,
761	        lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible,
762	        thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined,
763	        lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
764	
765	  % SOURCE: [Kleiman], "The Picard scheme", \S 2 (FGA Explained Ch.~9 \S 9.2),
766	  % Definitions df:aPf + df:Pfs (the absolute and relative Picard functors are
767	  % abelian-group-valued; the relative one is defined as a quotient of abelian
768	  % groups)
769	  % (read from references/kleiman-picard-src/kleiman-picard.tex, L1274-L1318).
770	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
771	  % "\begin{dfn}\label{df:aPf}
772	  %  The {\it absolute Picard functor} $\Pic_X$ is the functor from  the
773	  %  category of (locally Noetherian) $S$-schemes $T$ to the category of
774	  % abelian groups defined by the formula
775	  % 	$$\Pic_X(T):=\Pic(X_T).$$
776	  % \end{dfn}
777	  %  [\dots]
778	  % \begin{dfn}\label{df:Pfs}
779	  %   The {\it relative Picard functor} $\Pic_{X/S}$ is defined by
780	  % 	$$\Pic_{X/S}(T):=\Pic(X_T)\big/\Pic(T).$$
781	  %  Denote its associated sheaves in the Zariski, \'etale, and fppf
782	  % topologies  by
783	  % 	$$\Pic_{(X/S)\zar},\ \Pic_{(X/S)\et},\
784	  % 	\Pic_{(X/S)\fppf}.$$
785	  %  \end{dfn}"
786	  \textit{Source: [Kleiman], ``The Picard scheme'', \S 2,
787	  Defs.~df:aPf + df:Pfs (the target category is the category of abelian
788	  groups; the relative Picard functor is a quotient of abelian groups).}
789	  Let \(C/k\) be a smooth proper geometrically integral curve over a
790	  field \(k\), let \(\pi_C : C \to \Spec k\) be the structure morphism,
791	  and let \(T\) be a \(k\)-scheme with structure morphism
792	  \(\pi_T : T \to \Spec k\). The set
793	  \[
794	    \Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T) \;\;:=\;\; \Pic(C \times_k T) \,/\, \pi_T^*\Pic(T)
795	  \]
796	  of \cref{thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined} carries a canonical
797	  abelian-group structure with addition
798	  \(\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] + \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]
799	   := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L} \otimes_{C \times_k T} \mathcal{L}'\,\bigr]\),
800	  neutral element \(\bigl[\,\mathcal{O}_{C \times_k T}\,\bigr]\), and
801	  inverse
802	  \(-\bigl[\,\mathcal{L}\,\bigr] := \bigl[\,\mathcal{L}^{-1}\,\bigr]\),
803	  where \(\otimes_{C \times_k T}\) and \((-)^{-1}\) are the operations
804	  supplied by the substrate of \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
805	  restricted to the \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) carrier
806	  via \cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}. With this structure the quotient
807	  map
808	  \(\Pic(C \times_k T) \twoheadrightarrow \Pic^{\sharp}_{C/k}(T)\) is a
809	  surjective group homomorphism with kernel exactly \(\pi_T^*\Pic(T)\),
810	  in agreement with \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}.
811	\end{theorem}
812	
813	\begin{proof}
814	  \uses{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct,
815	        lem:pullback_compatible_with_tensorobj,
816	        lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,
817	        lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,
818	        lem:tensorobj_comm_iso,
819	        lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible,
820	        thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined,
821	        lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid}
822	  Write \(\Pic(C \times_k T)\) for the set of isomorphism classes of objects
823	  of \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) (line bundles on
824	  \(C \times_k T\)), with the operation
825	  \([L] + [L'] := [L \otimes_{C \times_k T} L']\). This operation is
826	  well-defined on isomorphism classes: an isomorphism \(L \cong L_0\) tensors
827	  to an isomorphism \(L \otimes L' \cong L_0 \otimes L'\) by functoriality of
828	  \(\otimes\) (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}), and
829	  symmetrically in the second argument. The group axioms are then
830	  \emph{exactly} the four existence-of-isomorphism facts established above,
831	  read on isomorphism classes:
832	  \begin{itemize}
833	    \item \textbf{associativity} \(([L]+[M])+[N] = [L]+([M]+[N])\) is
834	      \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso};
835	    \item \textbf{neutral element} \([\mathcal{O}_{C\times_k T}] + [L] = [L]\)
836	      (and on the right) is \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso};
837	    \item \textbf{commutativity} \([L]+[M]=[M]+[L]\) is
838	      \cref{lem:tensorobj_comm_iso};
839	    \item \textbf{inverse} \([L] + [L^{-1}] = [\mathcal{O}_{C\times_k T}]\),
840	      with \(L^{-1}=\mathcal{H}om(L,\mathcal{O}_{C\times_k T})\), is
841	      \cref{lem:tensorobj_inverse_invertible}.
842	  \end{itemize}
843	  Each axiom is a \emph{proposition} --- the existence of an isomorphism ---

1	# Analogy: the base-change comparison map for `PresheafOfModules.pullback` (the mate of pushforward's monoidal structure)
2	
3	## Mode
4	api-alignment
5	
6	## Slug
7	mate207
8	
9	## Iteration
10	207
11	
12	## Question
13	Lane TS needs, project-side, the comparison map
14	```
15	(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj (A ⊗ B)
16	   ⟶ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj A ⊗ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj B
17	```
18	`pullback φ := (pushforward φ).leftAdjoint` is an abstract left adjoint, so the map
19	must be constructed as the *mate* of `pushforward φ`'s monoidal structure. The planner
20	proposed building, project-side, a categorical lemma
21	`Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal : (F ⊣ G) → [G.LaxMonoidal] → F.OplaxMonoidal`
22	by dualizing the existing `rightAdjointLaxMonoidal`. Is that the aligned move, is the
23	dual genuinely absent, does the wiring line up, and is there a cheaper open-immersion route?
24	
25	## Project artifact(s)
26	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean` — `tensorObj_restrict_iso` and the
27	  whole TS cascade gated on this comparison map.
28	- Memory `ts-restrict-iso-residual.md` — prior iter-206 framing of the gap.
29	
30	## Decisions identified
31	
32	### Decision A: build `Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal` by dualizing `rightAdjointLaxMonoidal`?
33	
34	- **Mathlib idiom**: **THE LEMMA ALREADY EXISTS.**
35	  `CategoryTheory.Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal`
36	  (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Monoidal/Functor.lean:1009`):
37	  ```
38	  variable {F : C ⥤ D} {G : D ⥤ C} (adj : F ⊣ G) [G.LaxMonoidal]
39	  def leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal : F.OplaxMonoidal
40	  ```
41	  with oplax data
42	  - `η := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm (ε G)`
43	  - `δ X Y := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm ((adj.unit.app X ⊗ₘ adj.unit.app Y) ≫ μ G _ _)`
44	  and ALL coherence axioms (`δ_natural_left/right`, `oplax_associativity`,
45	  `oplax_{left,right}_unitality`) proven (lines 1012–1051). Companions:
46	  `Adjunction.IsMonoidal` instance (1053), `laxMonoidalEquivOplaxMonoidal` (1070),
47	  and `-isSimp` projection lemmas `leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal_η/_δ`. The dual it mirrors,
48	  `rightAdjointLaxMonoidal` (Functor.lean:897), is the *other* direction. Both ship in
49	  the same file; the planner's loogle name-search returned nothing only because of
50	  loogle name-substring quirks / a rate-limit hit, not absence.
51	- **Project's current path (proposed)**: re-derive the dual lemma project-side by
52	  dualizing `rightAdjointLaxMonoidal`'s diagram chase.
53	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost (re-inventing a shipped Mathlib lemma). Cost: a ~60-LOC
54	  oplax-coherence diagram chase that Mathlib already maintains, plus a parallel API that
55	  won't compose with `Adjunction.IsMonoidal` / `laxMonoidalEquivOplaxMonoidal`.
56	- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — do NOT build the dual; apply the existing
57	  `(pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ).leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal`. Its `δ` IS the required
58	  comparison map.
59	
60	### Decision B: where is the genuine project-side gap? `(pushforward φ).LaxMonoidal`.
61	
62	- **Adjunction wiring — present.** `PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ :
63	  pullback φ ⊣ pushforward φ` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Pullback.lean:50`)
64	  exists for the SAME `φ` whose `leftAdjoint` is `pullback φ` (`pullback φ :=
65	  (pushforward φ).leftAdjoint`, Pullback.lean:44). No reindexing mismatch. ✓
66	- **The hypothesis `leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal` needs is `[(pushforward φ).LaxMonoidal]`,
67	  and THAT is not in Mathlib as such.** The pushforward is a *composite*:
68	  `pushforward φ := pushforward₀ F R ⋙ restrictScalars φ`
69	  (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Presheaf/Pushforward.lean:86`).
70	  Mathlib's monoidal instance covers only the FIRST factor:
71	  `(pushforward₀OfCommRingCat F R).Monoidal`
72	  (`.../Presheaf/PushforwardZeroMonoidal.lean:33`), where
73	  `pushforward₀OfCommRingCat F R = pushforward₀ F (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)`
74	  (Pushforward.lean:68). There is NO monoidal/lax instance on the
75	  `restrictScalars φ` factor at the presheaf level, and none on `pushforward φ` itself.
76	- **The missing piece reduces to `(PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ).LaxMonoidal`.**
77	  Once present, Mathlib's `Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp` (Functor.lean:221) +
78	  `Functor.Monoidal.toLaxMonoidal` on `pushforward₀` assemble
79	  `(pushforward₀ F R ⋙ restrictScalars φ).LaxMonoidal`; a `CoreLaxMonoidal`/`inferInstanceAs`
80	  transport across the `pushforward φ` def-unfolding yields `(pushforward φ).LaxMonoidal`.
81	- **This presheaf-level lax instance is a sectionwise lift of an EXISTING ModuleCat lemma.**
82	  `ModuleCat.restrictScalars f` is already `LaxMonoidal`
83	  (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Monoidal/Adjunction.lean:102`), built precisely as
84	  `(extendRestrictScalarsAdj f).rightAdjointLaxMonoidal` — i.e. Mathlib uses the *same*
85	  mate idiom one level down. The presheaf monoidal structure is sectionwise
86	  (`(M₁ ⊗ M₂).obj X = M₁.obj X ⊗ M₂.obj X`, Presheaf/Monoidal.lean:46–81) and presheaf
87	  `restrictScalars φ` is sectionwise `ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app X)`, so the lax
88	  `μ`/`ε` glue sectionwise from the ModuleCat ones with naturality — a `CoreLaxMonoidal`
89	  build, NOT a new diagram chase.
90	- **Setup constraint (must hold in the project's statement):** the PresheafOfModules
91	  monoidal category requires CommRingCat-valued presheaves of rings (`R ⋙ forget₂ _ _`,
92	  Presheaf/Monoidal.lean:30), so both `R` and `S` must factor through CommRingCat and `φ`
93	  must be a morphism of presheaves of *commutative* rings. The project's `φ =
94	  (Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom f).hom` is structure-sheaf-valued (commutative), so this
95	  holds — but the instance must be stated over the CommRingCat-factored rings.
96	- **Gap**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (small, sectionwise) — one presheaf-level lax-monoidal
97	  instance for `restrictScalars`, mirroring an existing ModuleCat lemma.
98	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL** for `(PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ).LaxMonoidal`;
99	  everything downstream (`comp`, the mate, the δ map) is ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB / free.
100	
101	### Decision C: cheaper concrete open-immersion restriction route? (planner Q4)
102	
103	- Searched `Mathlib/.../SheafOfModules/*` and the ModuleCat tree for a concrete
104	  monoidal "restrict along an open immersion" / `Scheme.Modules.restrict` functor:
105	  **none found** (no `SheafOfModules` pullback/monoidal file; no `IsOpenImmersion`-keyed
106	  monoidal restriction in `Algebra/Category/ModuleCat`). Restriction to an open in this
107	  project still goes through the abstract `PresheafOfModules.pullback`.
108	- Since `leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal` already exists, the abstract route is now cheap (one
109	  sectionwise lax instance away), so there is no incentive to special-case open immersions.
110	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED** with the abstract route; no concrete shortcut exists or is needed.
111	
112	## Recommendation
113	Abandon the plan to build a `leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal` dual — it already ships in Mathlib
114	(`Functor.lean:1009`). The comparison map is literally
115	`Functor.OplaxMonoidal.δ (F := pullback φ)` once you write
116	`letI := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ).leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal`. The ONE genuine
117	project-side obligation is `(pushforward φ).LaxMonoidal`, which is NOT shipped because
118	`pushforward φ = pushforward₀ F R ⋙ restrictScalars φ` and only the `pushforward₀` factor
119	is monoidal in Mathlib. Provide `(PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ).LaxMonoidal` —
120	a sectionwise lift of the existing `ModuleCat.restrictScalars` lax-monoidal lemma
121	(Monoidal/Adjunction.lean:102), assembled as a `CoreLaxMonoidal` from the sectionwise
122	`ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app X)` data — then let `Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp` +
123	a defeq transport deliver `(pushforward φ).LaxMonoidal`. Estimated envelope: ~40–90 LOC
124	for the presheaf-level lax instance + ~10 LOC of plumbing (comp + transport + extract δ),
125	all over CommRingCat-factored presheaves of rings. This is a single small instance, not
126	a multi-file categorical build.
127	</content>
128	</invoke>

# `tensorObj_restrict_iso` — precise missing ingredient (iter-206 Lane TS)

## Target

```lean
noncomputable def tensorObj_restrict_iso {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X)
    [IsOpenImmersion f] (M N : X.Modules) :
    (tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f)
```
where `tensorObj M N := (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
(PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)`.

## Progress this iter (in the file, compiles GREEN)

The proof now performs **two real reduction steps** before the residual `sorry`:

1. `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback f` — reduces `· .restrict f` to
   `(pullback f).obj ·`. (Mathlib, `AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`.)
2. `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback f.toRingCatSheafHom` — **the key
   lemma surfaced this iter**: `sheafification ⋙ pullback φ ≅
   PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom ⋙ sheafification`
   (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackContinuous`). Moves the
   pullback *inside* the sheafification. This **discharges half (ii)** of the
   obstruction recorded in earlier iters ("sheafification commutes with
   pullback") — it IS in Mathlib, contrary to the old docstring claim.

After these the goal is the **purely presheaf-level** residual
```
sheafify ((PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj (M.val ⊗ N.val))
  ≅ (M.restrict f).tensorObj (N.restrict f)
```

## The single precise missing ingredient

A **`Monoidal` (or at least oplax-monoidal-with-iso-comparison) instance for
`PresheafOfModules.pullback φ`** w.r.t. `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj`,
i.e. the base-change isomorphism
```
(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj (A ⊗ B)
   ≅ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj A ⊗ (PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom).obj B.
```

### Why flatness does NOT shortcut this (correction to the iter-206 plan framing)

The plan's "re-scope to line bundles ⇒ flat-exactness closes it" addresses only
*iso-ness* of a comparison map. But `PresheafOfModules.pullback φ` is defined as
an **abstract left adjoint** `(pushforward φ).leftAdjoint`
(`Mathlib/.../Presheaf/Pullback.lean:44`) — there is **no sectionwise formula**,
so there is no canonical comparison map to even state, let alone prove iso. The
comparison map is the **oplax-monoidal structure of the left adjoint of the
(strong) monoidal `pushforward`** (doctrinal/mate of `pushforward`'s lax
structure). That oplax structure is what is absent from Mathlib; flatness would
only upgrade it from a map to an iso *after* it exists. So re-scoping the
signature to line bundles is **not** sufficient to unblock — the blocker is
upstream of the flatness step.

### What IS present in Mathlib (so the gap is "lift", not "invent")

- `ModuleCat.extendScalars` **is** `Monoidal`
  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction:42`; `μ`/`ε` via
  `AlgebraTensorModule` base-change isos). This is the *sectionwise* statement.
- `PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀OfCommRingCat` **is** `Monoidal`
  (`.../Presheaf/PushforwardZeroMonoidal.lean:33`) — but only because reindexing
  has `μIso = Iso.refl`; the extension-of-scalars lift is genuine work.
- `SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback`, `pullbackIso`,
  `Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app`, `hom_ext` (gluing) — all present.

### Suggested route for a future `mathlib-build` lane

Build, project-side, a `(PresheafOfModules.pullback φ).Monoidal` (or an explicit
`PresheafOfModules.pullbackMonoidalComparison` iso) by **lifting
`ModuleCat.extendScalars.Monoidal` sectionwise** through the
partial-left-adjoint construction, OR by the doctrinal-adjunction route
(left adjoint of strong-monoidal `pushforward` is oplax; strong because each
section is the `extendScalars` strong-monoidal comparison). Then
`tensorObj_restrict_iso` closes as
`sheafification.mapIso (pullbackMonoidalComparison …) ≪≫ (units bookkeeping)`,
where the units bookkeeping relates `sheafify (pullback M.val)` to
`M.restrict f` via `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`/`pullbackIso`.

This is the same *class* of multi-file Mathlib gap as the abandoned
`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` route, but **strictly smaller**: the
sectionwise ingredient already exists, and the sheafification half is now
discharged by `sheafificationCompPullback`.

## Downstream (all blocked on the above)

`tensorObj_assoc_iso`, `tensorObj_comm_iso`, the arbitrary-`L` unit iso,
`exists_tensorObj_inverse`, and `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` all consume
`tensorObj_restrict_iso` (global associator/unitor/braiding need it on a common
trivialising cover, glued via `isIso_iff_isIso_app`). They cannot close until

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Colimits
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Closed
10	
11	/-!
12	# The monoidal category structure on presheaves of modules
13	
14	Given a presheaf of commutative rings `R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat`, we construct
15	the monoidal category structure on the category of presheaves of modules
16	`PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)`. The tensor product `M₁ ⊗ M₂` is defined
17	as the presheaf of modules which sends `X : Cᵒᵖ` to `M₁.obj X ⊗ M₂.obj X`.
18	
19	## Notes
20	
21	This contribution was created as part of the AIM workshop
22	"Formalizing algebraic geometry" in June 2024.
23	
24	-/
25	
26	@[expose] public section
27	
28	open CategoryTheory MonoidalCategory BraidedCategory Category Limits
29	
30	universe v u v₁ u₁
31	
32	variable {C : Type*} [Category* C] {R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
33	
34	instance (X : Cᵒᵖ) : CommRing ((R ⋙ forget₂ _ RingCat).obj X) :=
35	  inferInstanceAs (CommRing (R.obj X))
36	
37	namespace PresheafOfModules
38	
39	namespace Monoidal
40	
41	variable (M₁ M₂ M₃ M₄ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
42	
43	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
44	/-- Auxiliary definition for `tensorObj`. -/
45	noncomputable def tensorObjMap {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) : M₁.obj X ⊗ M₂.obj X ⟶
46	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj (M₁.obj Y ⊗ M₂.obj Y) :=
47	  ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensorLift (fun m₁ m₂ ↦ M₁.map f m₁ ⊗ₜ M₂.map f m₂)
48	    (by
49	      intro m₁ m₁' m₂
50	      dsimp +instances
51	      rw [map_add, TensorProduct.add_tmul])
52	    (by intro a m₁ m₂; dsimp; erw [M₁.map_smul]; rfl)
53	    (by
54	      intro m₁ m₂ m₂'
55	      dsimp +instances
56	      rw [map_add, TensorProduct.tmul_add])
57	    (by intro a m₁ m₂; dsimp; erw [M₂.map_smul, TensorProduct.tmul_smul (r := R.map f a)]; rfl)
58	
59	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
60	/-- The tensor product of two presheaves of modules. -/
61	@[simps obj]
62	noncomputable def tensorObj : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _) where
63	  obj X := M₁.obj X ⊗ M₂.obj X
64	  map f := tensorObjMap M₁ M₂ f
65	  map_id X := ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (by
66	    intro m₁ m₂
67	    dsimp [tensorObjMap]
68	    simp
69	    rfl) -- `ModuleCat.restrictScalarsId'App_inv_apply` doesn't get picked up due to type mismatch
70	  map_comp f g := ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (by
71	    intro m₁ m₂
72	    dsimp [tensorObjMap]
73	    simp +instances)
74	
75	variable {M₁ M₂ M₃ M₄}
76	
77	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
78	@[simp]
79	lemma tensorObj_map_tmul {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y) (m₁ : M₁.obj X) (m₂ : M₂.obj X) :
80	    DFunLike.coe (α := (M₁.obj X ⊗ M₂.obj X :))
81	      (β := fun _ ↦ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj (M₁.obj Y ⊗ M₂.obj Y))
82	      (ModuleCat.Hom.hom (R := ↑(R.obj X)) ((tensorObj M₁ M₂).map f)) (m₁ ⊗ₜ[R.obj X] m₂) =
83	    M₁.map f m₁ ⊗ₜ[R.obj Y] M₂.map f m₂ := rfl
84	
85	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
86	/-- The tensor product of two morphisms of presheaves of modules. -/
87	@[simps]
88	noncomputable def tensorHom (f : M₁ ⟶ M₂) (g : M₃ ⟶ M₄) : tensorObj M₁ M₃ ⟶ tensorObj M₂ M₄ where
89	  app X := f.app X ⊗ₘ g.app X
90	  naturality {X Y} φ := ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun m₁ m₃ ↦ by
91	    dsimp
92	    rw [tensorObj_map_tmul]
93	    -- Need `erw` because of the type mismatch in `map` and the tensor product.
94	    erw [ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensorHom_tmul, tensorObj_map_tmul]
95	    rw [naturality_apply, naturality_apply]
96	    simp)
97	
98	end Monoidal
99	
100	open Monoidal
101	
102	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
103	open ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory in
104	noncomputable instance monoidalCategoryStruct :
105	    MonoidalCategoryStruct (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) where
106	  tensorObj := tensorObj
107	  whiskerLeft _ _ _ g := tensorHom (𝟙 _) g
108	  whiskerRight f _ := tensorHom f (𝟙 _)
109	  tensorHom := tensorHom
110	  tensorUnit := unit _
111	  associator M₁ M₂ M₃ := isoMk (fun _ ↦ α_ _ _ _)
112	    (fun _ _ _ ↦ ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext₃' (by intros; rfl))
113	  leftUnitor M := Iso.symm (isoMk (fun _ ↦ (λ_ _).symm) (fun X Y f ↦ by
114	    ext m
115	    dsimp [CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_obj]
116	    erw [leftUnitor_inv_apply, leftUnitor_inv_apply, tensorObj_map_tmul, (R.map f).hom.map_one]
117	    rfl))
118	  rightUnitor M := Iso.symm (isoMk (fun _ ↦ (ρ_ _).symm) (fun X Y f ↦ by
119	    ext m
120	    dsimp [CommRingCat.forgetToRingCat_obj]
121	    erw [rightUnitor_inv_apply, rightUnitor_inv_apply, tensorObj_map_tmul, (R.map f).hom.map_one]
122	    rfl))
123	
124	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
125	noncomputable instance monoidalCategory :
126	    MonoidalCategory (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) where
127	  tensorHom_def _ _ := by ext1; apply tensorHom_def
128	  id_tensorHom_id _ _ := by ext1; apply id_tensorHom_id
129	  tensorHom_comp_tensorHom _ _ _ _ := by ext1; apply tensorHom_comp_tensorHom
130	  whiskerLeft_id M₁ M₂ := by
131	    ext1 X
132	    apply MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft_id (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X))
133	  id_whiskerRight _ _ := by
134	    ext1 X
135	    apply MonoidalCategory.id_whiskerRight (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X))
136	  associator_naturality _ _ _ := by ext1; apply associator_naturality
137	  leftUnitor_naturality _ := by ext1; apply leftUnitor_naturality
138	  rightUnitor_naturality _ := by ext1; apply rightUnitor_naturality
139	  pentagon _ _ _ _ := by ext1; apply pentagon
140	  triangle _ _ := by ext1; apply triangle
141	
142	open BraidedCategory
143	
144	instance symmetricCategory :
145	    SymmetricCategory (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) where
146	  braiding M₁ M₂ :=
147	    isoMk (fun X ↦ braiding (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X))
148	      (fun _ _ f ↦ ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun _ _ ↦ rfl))
149	  braiding_naturality_right _ _ _ _ := by
150	    ext : 1
151	    exact ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun _ _ ↦ rfl)
152	  braiding_naturality_left _ _ := by
153	    ext : 1
154	    exact ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun _ _ ↦ rfl)
155	  hexagon_forward _ _ _ := by
156	    ext : 1
157	    apply hexagon_forward (C := ModuleCat (R.obj _))
158	  hexagon_reverse _ _ _ := by
159	    ext : 1
160	    apply hexagon_reverse (C := ModuleCat (R.obj _))
161	  symmetry _ _ := by
162	    ext : 1
163	    apply SymmetricCategory.symmetry (C := ModuleCat (R.obj _))
164	
165	section
166	
167	variable (M₁ M₂ M₃ M₄ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))
168	
169	lemma tensorObj_obj (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
170	    (M₁ ⊗ M₂).obj X =
171	      MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X) := rfl
172	
173	attribute [local simp] tensorObj_obj
174	
175	variable {M₂ M₃} in
176	@[simp]
177	lemma whiskerLeft_app (f : M₂ ⟶ M₃) (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
178	    dsimp% (M₁ ◁ f).app X = whiskerLeft (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X) (f.app X) :=
179	  rfl
180	
181	variable {M₁ M₂} in
182	@[simp]
183	lemma whiskerRight_app (f : M₁ ⟶ M₂) (M₃ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
184	    dsimp% (f ▷ M₃).app X = whiskerRight (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (f.app X) (M₃.obj X) := rfl
185	
186	variable {M₁ M₂ M₃ M₄} in
187	@[simp]
188	lemma tensorHom_app (f : M₁ ⟶ M₂) (g : M₃ ⟶ M₄) (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
189	    dsimp% (f ⊗ₘ g).app X =
190	      MonoidalCategory.tensorHom (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (f.app X) (g.app X) := rfl
191	
192	@[simp]
193	lemma leftUnitor_hom_app (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
194	    dsimp% (λ_ M₁).hom.app X = (leftUnitor (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X)).hom :=
195	  rfl
196	
197	@[simp]
198	lemma leftUnitor_inv_app (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
199	    dsimp% (λ_ M₁).inv.app X = (leftUnitor (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X)).inv := by
200	  rfl
201	
202	@[simp]
203	lemma rightUnitor_hom_app (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
204	    dsimp% (ρ_ M₁).hom.app X = (rightUnitor (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X)).hom :=
205	  rfl
206	
207	@[simp]
208	lemma rightUnitor_inv_app (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
209	    dsimp% (ρ_ M₁).inv.app X = (rightUnitor (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X)).inv :=
210	  rfl
211	
212	@[simp]
213	lemma associator_hom_app (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
214	    (α_ M₁ M₂ M₃).hom.app X =
215	      (associator (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X) (M₃.obj X)).hom :=
216	  rfl
217

219	lemma associator_inv_app (X : Cᵒᵖ) :
220	    (α_ M₁ M₂ M₃).inv.app X =
221	      (associator (C := ModuleCat (R.obj X)) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X) (M₃.obj X)).inv :=

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.ChangeOfRings
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf
10	
11	/-!
12	# Change of presheaf of rings
13	
14	In this file, we define the restriction of scalars functor
15	`restrictScalars α : PresheafOfModules.{v} R' ⥤ PresheafOfModules.{v} R`
16	attached to a morphism of presheaves of rings `α : R ⟶ R'`.
17	
18	-/
19	
20	@[expose] public section
21	
22	universe v v' u u'
23	
24	open CategoryTheory
25	
26	namespace PresheafOfModules
27	
28	variable {C : Type u'} [Category.{v'} C] {R R' : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
29	
30	/-- The restriction of scalars of presheaves of modules, on objects. -/
31	@[simps]
32	noncomputable def restrictScalarsObj (M' : PresheafOfModules.{v} R') (α : R ⟶ R') :
33	    PresheafOfModules R where
34	  obj := fun X ↦ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom).obj (M'.obj X)
35	  -- TODO: after https://github.com/leanprover-community/mathlib4/pull/19511 we need to hint `(X := ...)` and `(Y := ...)`.
36	  -- This suggests `restrictScalars` needs to be redesigned.
37	  map := fun {X Y} f ↦ ModuleCat.ofHom
38	      (X := (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom).obj (M'.obj X))
39	      (Y := (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (R.map f).hom).obj
40	        ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Y).hom).obj (M'.obj Y)))
41	    { toFun := M'.map f
42	      map_add' := map_add _
43	      map_smul' := fun r x ↦ (M'.map_smul f (α.app _ r) x).trans (by
44	        have eq := RingHom.congr_fun (congrArg RingCat.Hom.hom <| α.naturality f) r
45	        dsimp at eq
46	        rw [← eq]
47	        rfl) }
48	
49	/-- The restriction of scalars functor `PresheafOfModules R' ⥤ PresheafOfModules R`
50	induced by a morphism of presheaves of rings `R ⟶ R'`. -/
51	@[simps]
52	noncomputable def restrictScalars (α : R ⟶ R') :
53	    PresheafOfModules.{v} R' ⥤ PresheafOfModules.{v} R where
54	  obj M' := M'.restrictScalarsObj α
55	  map φ' :=
56	    { app := fun X ↦ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom).map (Hom.app φ' X)
57	      naturality := fun {X Y} f ↦ by
58	        ext x
59	        exact naturality_apply φ' f x }
60	
61	instance (α : R ⟶ R') : (restrictScalars.{v} α).Additive where
62	
63	instance : (restrictScalars (𝟙 R)).Full := inferInstanceAs (𝟭 _).Full
64	
65	instance (α : R ⟶ R') : (restrictScalars α).Faithful where
66	  map_injective h := (toPresheaf R').map_injective ((toPresheaf R).congr_map h)
67	
68	/-- The isomorphism `restrictScalars α ⋙ toPresheaf R ≅ toPresheaf R'` for any
69	morphism of presheaves of rings `α : R ⟶ R'`. -/
70	noncomputable def restrictScalarsCompToPresheaf (α : R ⟶ R') :
71	    restrictScalars.{v} α ⋙ toPresheaf R ≅ toPresheaf R' := Iso.refl _
72	
73	end PresheafOfModules
74

10	/-!
11	# Pushforward of presheaves of modules
12	
13	If `F : C ⥤ D`, the precomposition `F.op ⋙ _` induces a functor from presheaves
14	over `D` to presheaves over `C`. When `R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat`, we define the
15	induced functor `pushforward₀ : PresheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ PresheafOfModules.{v} (F.op ⋙ R)`
16	on presheaves of modules.
17	
18	In case we have a morphism of presheaves of rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, we also construct
19	a functor `pushforward : PresheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ PresheafOfModules.{v} S`, and
20	we show that they interact with the composition of morphisms similarly as pseudofunctors.
21	
22	-/
23	
24	@[expose] public section
25	
26	universe v v₁ v₂ v₃ v₄ u₁ u₂ u₃ u₄ u
27	
28	open CategoryTheory Functor
29	
30	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
31	  {E : Type u₃} [Category.{v₃} E] {E' : Type u₄} [Category.{v₄} E']
32	
33	namespace PresheafOfModules
34	
35	variable (F : C ⥤ D)
36	
37	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
38	/-- Implementation of `pushforward₀`. -/
39	@[simps]
40	def pushforward₀_obj (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) (M : PresheafOfModules R) :
41	    PresheafOfModules (F.op ⋙ R) :=
42	  { obj X := ModuleCat.of _ (M.obj (F.op.obj X))
43	    map {X Y} f := M.map (F.op.map f)
44	    map_id X := by
45	      refine ModuleCat.hom_ext
46	        -- Work around an instance diamond for `restrictScalarsId'`
47	        (@LinearMap.ext _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (_) (_) _ _ _ (fun x => ?_))
48	      exact (M.congr_map_apply (F.op.map_id X) x).trans (by simp)
49	    map_comp := fun f g ↦ by
50	      refine ModuleCat.hom_ext
51	        -- Work around an instance diamond for `restrictScalarsId'`
52	        (@LinearMap.ext _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (_) (_) _ _ _ (fun x => ?_))
53	      exact (M.congr_map_apply (F.op.map_comp f g) x).trans (by simp) }
54	
55	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
56	/-- The pushforward functor on presheaves of modules for a functor `F : C ⥤ D` and
57	`R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat`. On the underlying presheaves of abelian groups, it is induced
58	by the precomposition with `F.op`. -/
59	def pushforward₀ (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
60	    PresheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ PresheafOfModules.{v} (F.op ⋙ R) where
61	  obj M := pushforward₀_obj F R M
62	  map {M₁ M₂} φ := { app X := φ.app _ }
63	
64	/-- If `F : C ⥤ D` if a functor and `R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat` is a presheaf
65	of commutative rings, this is the pushforward functor from the category
66	of presheaves of modules on `R` to the category of presheaves of
67	modules on `F.op ⋙ R`. -/
68	abbrev pushforward₀OfCommRingCat (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}) :
69	    PresheafOfModules.{v} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _) ⥤
70	      PresheafOfModules.{v} ((F.op ⋙ R) ⋙ forget₂ _ _) :=
71	  pushforward₀ F (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)
72	
73	/-- The pushforward of presheaves of modules commutes with the forgetful functor
74	to presheaves of abelian groups. -/
75	noncomputable def pushforward₀CompToPresheaf (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
76	    pushforward₀.{v} F R ⋙ toPresheaf _ ≅ toPresheaf _ ⋙ (whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj F.op :=
77	  Iso.refl _
78	
79	variable {F}
80	variable {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)
81	
82	attribute [local simp] pushforward₀ in
83	/-- The pushforward functor `PresheafOfModules R ⥤ PresheafOfModules S` induced by
84	a morphism of presheaves of rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`. -/
85	@[simps! obj_obj]
86	noncomputable def pushforward : PresheafOfModules.{v} R ⥤ PresheafOfModules.{v} S :=
87	  pushforward₀ F R ⋙ restrictScalars φ
88	
89	/-- The pushforward of presheaves of modules commutes with the forgetful functor
90	to presheaves of abelian groups. -/
91	noncomputable def pushforwardCompToPresheaf :
92	    pushforward.{v} φ ⋙ toPresheaf _ ≅ toPresheaf _ ⋙ (whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj F.op :=
93	  Iso.refl _
94	
95	lemma pushforward_obj_map_apply (M : PresheafOfModules.{v} R) {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y)
96	    (m : (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app X).hom).obj (M.obj (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)))) :
97	      (((pushforward φ).obj M).map f).hom m = M.map (F.map f.unop).op m := rfl
98	
99	/-- `@[simp]`-normal form of `pushforward_obj_map_apply`. -/
100	@[simp]
101	lemma pushforward_obj_map_apply' (M : PresheafOfModules.{v} R) {X Y : Cᵒᵖ} (f : X ⟶ Y)
102	    (m : (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app X).hom).obj (M.obj (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)))) :
103	      DFunLike.coe
104	        (F := ↑((ModuleCat.restrictScalars _).obj _) →ₗ[_]
105	          ↑((ModuleCat.restrictScalars (S.map f).hom).obj ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars _).obj _)))
106	        (((pushforward φ).obj M).map f).hom m = M.map (F.map f.unop).op m := rfl
107	
108	lemma pushforward_map_app_apply {M N : PresheafOfModules.{v} R} (α : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
109	    (m : (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app X).hom).obj (M.obj (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)))) :
110	    (((pushforward φ).map α).app X).hom m = α.app (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)) m := rfl
111	
112	/-- `@[simp]`-normal form of `pushforward_map_app_apply`. -/
113	@[simp]
114	lemma pushforward_map_app_apply' {M N : PresheafOfModules.{v} R} (α : M ⟶ N) (X : Cᵒᵖ)
115	    (m : (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (φ.app X).hom).obj (M.obj (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)))) :
116	    DFunLike.coe
117	      (F := ↑((ModuleCat.restrictScalars _).obj _) →ₗ[_] ↑((ModuleCat.restrictScalars _).obj _))
118	      (((pushforward φ).map α).app X).hom m = α.app (Opposite.op (F.obj X.unop)) m := rfl
119	
120	section
121	
122	variable (R) in
123	/-- The pushforward functor by the identity morphism identifies to
124	the identify functor of the category of presheaves of modules. -/
125	noncomputable def pushforwardId :
126	    pushforward.{v} (S := R) (F := 𝟭 _) (𝟙 R) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
127	  Iso.refl _
128	
129	section
130	
131	variable {T : Eᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {G : D ⥤ E} (ψ : R ⟶ G.op ⋙ T)
132	
133	/-- The composition of two pushforward functors on categories of presheaves of modules
134	identify to the pushforward for the composition. -/
135	noncomputable def pushforwardComp :
136	    pushforward.{v} ψ ⋙ pushforward.{v} φ ≅
137	      pushforward.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ whiskerLeft F.op ψ) :=
138	  Iso.refl _
139	
140	variable {T' : E'ᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {G' : E ⥤ E'} (ψ' : T ⟶ G'.op ⋙ T')
141	
142	lemma pushforward_assoc :
143	    (pushforward ψ').isoWhiskerLeft (pushforwardComp φ ψ) ≪≫
144	      pushforwardComp (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ F.op.whiskerLeft ψ) ψ' =
145	    ((pushforward ψ').associator (pushforward ψ) (pushforward φ)).symm ≪≫
146	      isoWhiskerRight (pushforwardComp ψ ψ') (pushforward φ) ≪≫
147	        pushforwardComp (G := G ⋙ G') φ (ψ ≫ G.op.whiskerLeft ψ') := by ext; rfl
148	
149	end
150	
151	lemma pushforward_comp_id :
152	    pushforwardComp.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S) φ =
153	      isoWhiskerLeft (pushforward.{v} φ) (pushforwardId S) ≪≫ rightUnitor _ := by ext; rfl
154	
155	lemma pushforward_id_comp :
156	    pushforwardComp.{v} (G := 𝟭 _) φ (𝟙 R) =
157	      isoWhiskerRight (pushforwardId R) (pushforward.{v} φ) ≪≫ leftUnitor _ := by ext; rfl
158	
159	end
160	
161	end PresheafOfModules
162

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.ChangeOfRings
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Basic
10	
11	/-!
12	# The monoidal adjunction between the extension and the restriction of scalars
13	
14	Let `f : R →+* S` be a morphism of commutative rings. We show that the functor
15	`extendsScalars f : ModuleCat R ⥤ ModuleCat S` is monoidal, and deduce that
16	`restrictScalars f : ModuleCat S ⥤ ModuleCat R` is lax monoidal.
17	
18	-/
19	
20	@[expose] public section
21	
22	universe u
23	
24	open CategoryTheory ModuleCat MonoidalCategory Limits
25	  Functor.LaxMonoidal Functor.OplaxMonoidal TensorProduct
26	
27	namespace ModuleCat
28	
29	variable {R S : Type u} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] (f : R →+* S)
30	
31	@[simp]
32	lemma extendsScalars_map_leftUnitor_inv_one_tmul (M : ModuleCat R) (m : M) :
33	    letI := f.toAlgebra
34	    (extendScalars f).map (λ_ M).inv ((1 : S) ⊗ₜ[R] m) = (1 : S) ⊗ₜ[R] (1 ⊗ₜ m) := rfl
35	
36	@[simp]
37	lemma extendsScalars_map_rightUnitor_inv_one_tmul (M : ModuleCat R) (m : M) :
38	    letI := f.toAlgebra
39	    (extendScalars f).map (ρ_ M).inv ((1 : S) ⊗ₜ[R] m) = (1 : S) ⊗ₜ[R] (m ⊗ₜ 1) := rfl
40	
41	open ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory in
42	noncomputable instance : (extendScalars f).Monoidal :=
43	  letI : Algebra R S := f.toAlgebra
44	  Functor.CoreMonoidal.toMonoidal
45	    (.mk'
46	      (εIso := (AlgebraTensorModule.rid R S S).symm.toModuleIso)
47	      (μIso := fun M₁ M₂ ↦ (AlgebraTensorModule.distribBaseChange R S M₁ M₂).symm.toModuleIso)
48	      (μIso_inv_natural_left := fun {M₁ M₁'} g M₂ ↦
49	        ((extendRestrictScalarsAdj f).homEquiv _ _).injective
50	          (tensor_ext (fun _ _ ↦ rfl)))
51	      (μIso_inv_natural_right := fun {M₂ M₂'} M₁ g ↦
52	        ((extendRestrictScalarsAdj f).homEquiv _ _).injective
53	          (tensor_ext (fun _ _ ↦ rfl)))
54	      (oplax_associativity := fun M₁ M₂ M₃ ↦
55	        ((extendRestrictScalarsAdj f).homEquiv _ _).injective
56	          (tensor_ext₃' (fun _ _ _ ↦ rfl)))
57	      (oplax_left_unitality := fun M ↦ by
58	        ext m
59	        dsimp
60	        rw [MonoidalCategory.leftUnitor_inv_apply]
61	        erw [AlgebraTensorModule.distribBaseChange_tmul,
62	          MonoidalCategory.whiskerRight_apply,
63	          AlgebraTensorModule.rid_tmul]
64	        rw [one_smul]
65	        rfl)
66	      (oplax_right_unitality := fun M ↦ by
67	        ext m
68	        dsimp
69	        rw [MonoidalCategory.rightUnitor_inv_apply]
70	        erw [AlgebraTensorModule.distribBaseChange_tmul,
71	          MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft_apply,
72	          AlgebraTensorModule.rid_tmul]
73	        rw [one_smul]
74	        rfl))
75	
76	lemma extendScalars_ε :
77	    letI := f.toAlgebra
78	    dsimp% ε (extendScalars f) = (AlgebraTensorModule.rid R S S).toModuleIso.inv := rfl
79	
80	lemma extendScalars_η :
81	    letI := f.toAlgebra
82	    dsimp% η (extendScalars f) = (AlgebraTensorModule.rid R S S).toModuleIso.hom := rfl
83	
84	lemma extendScalars_μ (M₁ M₂ : ModuleCat R) :
85	    letI := f.toAlgebra
86	    dsimp% μ (extendScalars f) M₁ M₂ =
87	      (AlgebraTensorModule.distribBaseChange R S M₁ M₂).toModuleIso.inv :=
88	  rfl
89	
90	lemma extendScalars_δ (M₁ M₂ : ModuleCat R) :
91	    letI := f.toAlgebra
92	    dsimp% δ (extendScalars f) M₁ M₂ =
93	      (AlgebraTensorModule.distribBaseChange R S M₁ M₂).toModuleIso.hom :=
94	  rfl
95	
96	@[simp]
97	lemma extendScalars_δ_tmul (M₁ M₂ : ModuleCat R) (m₁ : M₁) (m₂ : M₂) :
98	    letI := f.toAlgebra
99	    dsimp% δ (extendScalars f) M₁ M₂ (((1 : S) ⊗ₜ[R] (m₁ ⊗ₜ[R] m₂) :)) =
100	      ((1 : S) ⊗ₜ[R] m₁) ⊗ₜ[S] ((1 : S) ⊗ₜ[R] m₂) := rfl
101	
102	noncomputable instance : (restrictScalars f).LaxMonoidal :=
103	  (extendRestrictScalarsAdj f).rightAdjointLaxMonoidal
104	
105	@[simp]
106	lemma restrictScalars_η (r : R) :
107	    ε (restrictScalars f) r = f r := by
108	  letI := f.toAlgebra
109	  dsimp [Adjunction.rightAdjointLaxMonoidal_ε]
110	  rw [extendRestrictScalarsAdj_homEquiv_apply, extendScalars_η]
111	  erw [AlgebraTensorModule.rid_tmul]
112	  rw [RingHom.smul_toAlgebra, mul_one]
113	
114	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
115	@[simp]
116	lemma restrictScalars_μ_tmul (M₁ M₂ : ModuleCat S) (m₁ : M₁) (m₂ : M₂) :
117	    dsimp% μ (restrictScalars f) M₁ M₂ (m₁ ⊗ₜ m₂) = m₁ ⊗ₜ m₂ := by
118	  dsimp [Adjunction.rightAdjointLaxMonoidal_μ]
119	  rw [extendRestrictScalarsAdj_homEquiv_apply]
120	  dsimp
121	  rw [extendScalars_δ_tmul, tensorHom_tmul,
122	    extendRestrictScalarsAdj_counit_app_apply_one_tmul,
123	    extendRestrictScalarsAdj_counit_app_apply_one_tmul]
124	
125	end ModuleCat
126

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Monoidal
9	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Pushforward
10	
11	/-!
12	# The pushforward functor is monoidal
13	
14	If `F : C ⥤ D` is a functor and `R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat` is a presheaf
15	of commutative rings, then the pushforward functor from the category
16	of presheaves of modules on `R` to the category of presheaves of
17	modules on `F.op ⋙ R` is monoidal.
18	
19	-/
20	
21	@[expose] public section
22	
23	universe v u
24	
25	open CategoryTheory MonoidalCategory
26	
27	namespace PresheafOfModules
28	
29	variable {C D : Type*} [Category* C] [Category* D]
30	  (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u})
31	
32	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
33	noncomputable instance : (pushforward₀OfCommRingCat F R).Monoidal :=
34	  Functor.CoreMonoidal.toMonoidal
35	    { εIso := Iso.refl _
36	      μIso _ _ := Iso.refl _
37	      left_unitality _ := by rw [← cancel_epi (λ_ _).inv]; cat_disch
38	      right_unitality _ := by rw [← cancel_epi (ρ_ _).inv]; cat_disch }
39	
40	end PresheafOfModules

548:    (@Monoidal.toLaxMonoidal _ _ _ _ _ _ _ : F.Monoidal → F.LaxMonoidal) := by
657:def toLaxMonoidal : F.LaxMonoidal where
687:@[simps! toLaxMonoidal toOplaxMonoidal, implicit_reducible]
689:  toLaxMonoidal := h.toLaxMonoidal

958:    letI := adj.rightAdjointLaxMonoidal
960:  letI := adj.rightAdjointLaxMonoidal
1009:def leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal : F.OplaxMonoidal where
1054:    letI := adj.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal
1056:  letI := adj.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal
1058:  · simp [homEquiv_counit, leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal_η]
1059:  · simp [homEquiv_counit, ← μ_natural, leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal_δ]
1063:attribute [local simp] leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal_η leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal_δ

1067:`Adjunction.rightAdjointLaxMonoidal`, because compatibilities between
1071:  toFun _ := adj.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal
1072:  invFun _ := adj.rightAdjointLaxMonoidal

1257:structure LaxMonoidalFunctor extends C ⥤ D where

55	namespace Functor
56	
57	-- The direction of `left_unitality` and `right_unitality` as simp lemmas may look strange:
58	-- remember the rule of thumb that component indices of natural transformations
59	-- "weigh more" than structural maps.
60	-- (However by this argument `associativity` is currently stated backwards!)
61	/-- A functor `F : C ⥤ D` between monoidal categories is lax monoidal if it is
62	equipped with morphisms `ε : 𝟙_ D ⟶ F.obj (𝟙_ C)` and `μ X Y : F.obj X ⊗ F.obj Y ⟶ F.obj (X ⊗ Y)`,
63	satisfying the appropriate coherences. -/
64	@[ext]
65	class LaxMonoidal (F : C ⥤ D) where
66	  /-- the unit morphism of a lax monoidal functor -/
67	  ε (F) : 𝟙_ D ⟶ F.obj (𝟙_ C)
68	  /-- the tensorator of a lax monoidal functor -/
69	  μ (F) : ∀ X Y : C, F.obj X ⊗ F.obj Y ⟶ F.obj (X ⊗ Y)
70	  μ_natural_left (F) :
71	    ∀ {X Y : C} (f : X ⟶ Y) (X' : C),
72	      F.map f ▷ F.obj X' ≫ μ Y X' = μ X X' ≫ F.map (f ▷ X') := by
73	    cat_disch
74	  μ_natural_right (F) :
75	    ∀ {X Y : C} (X' : C) (f : X ⟶ Y),
76	      F.obj X' ◁ F.map f ≫ μ X' Y = μ X' X ≫ F.map (X' ◁ f) := by
77	    cat_disch
78	  /-- associativity of the tensorator -/
79	  associativity (F) :
80	    ∀ X Y Z : C,
81	      μ X Y ▷ F.obj Z ≫ μ (X ⊗ Y) Z ≫ F.map (α_ X Y Z).hom =
82	        (α_ (F.obj X) (F.obj Y) (F.obj Z)).hom ≫ F.obj X ◁ μ Y Z ≫ μ X (Y ⊗ Z) := by
83	    cat_disch
84	  -- unitality
85	  left_unitality (F) :
86	    ∀ X : C, (λ_ (F.obj X)).hom = ε ▷ F.obj X ≫ μ (𝟙_ C) X ≫ F.map (λ_ X).hom := by
87	      cat_disch
88	  right_unitality (F) :
89	    ∀ X : C, (ρ_ (F.obj X)).hom = F.obj X ◁ ε ≫ μ X (𝟙_ C) ≫ F.map (ρ_ X).hom := by
90	    cat_disch
91	
92	namespace LaxMonoidal
93	
94	attribute [reassoc (attr := simp)] μ_natural_left μ_natural_right
95	  associativity
96	
97	attribute [simp, reassoc] right_unitality left_unitality
98	
99	section
100	
101	variable (F : C ⥤ D) [F.LaxMonoidal]
102	
103	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
104	theorem μ_natural {X Y X' Y' : C} (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : X' ⟶ Y') :
105	    (F.map f ⊗ₘ F.map g) ≫ μ F Y Y' = μ F X X' ≫ F.map (f ⊗ₘ g) := by
106	  simp [tensorHom_def]
107	
108	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
109	theorem left_unitality_inv (X : C) :
110	    (λ_ (F.obj X)).inv ≫ ε F ▷ F.obj X ≫ μ F (𝟙_ C) X = F.map (λ_ X).inv := by
111	  rw [Iso.inv_comp_eq, left_unitality, Category.assoc, Category.assoc, ← F.map_comp,
112	    Iso.hom_inv_id, F.map_id, comp_id]
113	
114	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
115	theorem right_unitality_inv (X : C) :
116	    (ρ_ (F.obj X)).inv ≫ F.obj X ◁ ε F ≫ μ F X (𝟙_ C) = F.map (ρ_ X).inv := by
117	  rw [Iso.inv_comp_eq, right_unitality, Category.assoc, Category.assoc, ← F.map_comp,
118	    Iso.hom_inv_id, F.map_id, comp_id]
119	
120	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
121	theorem associativity_inv (X Y Z : C) :
122	    F.obj X ◁ μ F Y Z ≫ μ F X (Y ⊗ Z) ≫ F.map (α_ X Y Z).inv =
123	      (α_ (F.obj X) (F.obj Y) (F.obj Z)).inv ≫ μ F X Y ▷ F.obj Z ≫ μ F (X ⊗ Y) Z := by
124	  rw [Iso.eq_inv_comp, ← associativity_assoc, ← F.map_comp, Iso.hom_inv_id,
125	    F.map_id, comp_id]
126	
127	@[reassoc]
128	lemma ε_tensorHom_comp_μ {X : C} {Y : D} (f : Y ⟶ F.obj X) :
129	    (ε F ⊗ₘ f) ≫ μ F (𝟙_ C) X = 𝟙_ D ◁ f ≫ (λ_ (F.obj X)).hom ≫ F.map (λ_ X).inv := by
130	  simp [tensorHom_def']
131	
132	@[reassoc]
133	lemma tensorHom_ε_comp_μ {X : C} {Y : D} (f : Y ⟶ F.obj X) :
134	    (f ⊗ₘ ε F) ≫ μ F X (𝟙_ C) = f ▷ 𝟙_ D ≫ (ρ_ (F.obj X)).hom ≫ F.map (ρ_ X).inv := by
135	  simp [tensorHom_def]
136	
137	@[reassoc]
138	lemma tensorUnit_whiskerLeft_comp_leftUnitor_hom {X : C} {Y : D} (f : Y ⟶ F.obj X) :
139	    𝟙_ D ◁ f ≫ (λ_ (F.obj X)).hom = (ε F ⊗ₘ f) ≫ μ F (𝟙_ C) X ≫ F.map (λ_ X).hom := by
140	  simp [tensorHom_def']
141	
142	@[reassoc]
143	lemma whiskerRight_tensorUnit_comp_rightUnitor_hom {X : C} {Y : D} (f : Y ⟶ F.obj X) :
144	    f ▷ 𝟙_ D ≫ (ρ_ (F.obj X)).hom = (f ⊗ₘ ε F) ≫ μ F X (𝟙_ C) ≫ F.map (ρ_ X).hom := by
145	  simp [tensorHom_def]
146	
147	@[reassoc]
148	lemma μ_whiskerRight_comp_μ (X Y Z : C) :
149	    μ F X Y ▷ F.obj Z ≫ μ F (X ⊗ Y) Z = (α_ (F.obj X) (F.obj Y) (F.obj Z)).hom ≫
150	      F.obj X ◁ μ F Y Z ≫ μ F X (Y ⊗ Z) ≫ F.map (α_ X Y Z).inv := by
151	  rw [← associativity_assoc, ← F.map_comp, Iso.hom_inv_id, map_id, Category.comp_id]
152	
153	@[reassoc]
154	lemma whiskerLeft_μ_comp_μ (X Y Z : C) :
155	    F.obj X ◁ μ F Y Z ≫ μ F X (Y ⊗ Z) = (α_ (F.obj X) (F.obj Y) (F.obj Z)).inv ≫
156	      μ F X Y ▷ F.obj Z ≫ μ F (X ⊗ Y) Z ≫ F.map (α_ X Y Z).hom := by
157	  rw [associativity, Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc]
158	
159	end

163	variable {F : C ⥤ D}
164	    /- unit morphism -/
165	    (ε : 𝟙_ D ⟶ F.obj (𝟙_ C))
166	    /- tensorator -/
167	    (μ : ∀ X Y : C, F.obj X ⊗ F.obj Y ⟶ F.obj (X ⊗ Y))
168	    (μ_natural :
169	      ∀ {X Y X' Y' : C} (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : X' ⟶ Y'),
170	        (F.map f ⊗ₘ F.map g) ≫ μ Y Y' = μ X X' ≫ F.map (f ⊗ₘ g) := by
171	      cat_disch)
172	    /- associativity of the tensorator -/
173	    (associativity :
174	      ∀ X Y Z : C,
175	        (μ X Y ⊗ₘ 𝟙 (F.obj Z)) ≫ μ (X ⊗ Y) Z ≫ F.map (α_ X Y Z).hom =
176	          (α_ (F.obj X) (F.obj Y) (F.obj Z)).hom ≫ (𝟙 (F.obj X) ⊗ₘ μ Y Z) ≫ μ X (Y ⊗ Z) := by
177	      cat_disch)
178	    /- unitality -/
179	    (left_unitality :
180	      ∀ X : C, (λ_ (F.obj X)).hom = (ε ⊗ₘ 𝟙 (F.obj X)) ≫ μ (𝟙_ C) X ≫ F.map (λ_ X).hom := by
181	        cat_disch)
182	    (right_unitality :
183	      ∀ X : C, (ρ_ (F.obj X)).hom = (𝟙 (F.obj X) ⊗ₘ ε) ≫ μ X (𝟙_ C) ≫ F.map (ρ_ X).hom := by
184	        cat_disch)

184	        cat_disch)

188	/--
189	A constructor for lax monoidal functors whose axioms are described by `tensorHom` instead of
190	`whiskerLeft` and `whiskerRight`.
191	-/
192	@[implicit_reducible]
193	def ofTensorHom : F.LaxMonoidal where
194	  ε := ε
195	  μ := μ
196	  μ_natural_left := fun f X' => by
197	    simp_rw [← tensorHom_id, ← F.map_id, μ_natural]
198	  μ_natural_right := fun X' f => by
199	    simp_rw [← id_tensorHom, ← F.map_id, μ_natural]
200	  associativity := fun X Y Z => by
201	    simp_rw [← tensorHom_id, ← id_tensorHom, associativity]
202	  left_unitality := fun X => by
203	    simp_rw [← tensorHom_id, left_unitality]
204	  right_unitality := fun X => by
205	    simp_rw [← id_tensorHom, right_unitality]
206	
207	end
208	
209	@[simps]
210	instance id : (𝟭 C).LaxMonoidal where
211	  ε := 𝟙 _
212	  μ _ _ := 𝟙 _

215	
216	variable (F : C ⥤ D) (G : D ⥤ E)
217	
218	variable [F.LaxMonoidal] [G.LaxMonoidal]
219	
220	@[simps]
221	instance comp : (F ⋙ G).LaxMonoidal where
222	  ε := ε G ≫ G.map (ε F)
223	  μ X Y := μ G _ _ ≫ G.map (μ F X Y)
224	  μ_natural_left _ _ := by
225	    simp_rw [comp_obj, F.comp_map, μ_natural_left_assoc, assoc, ← G.map_comp, μ_natural_left]
226	  μ_natural_right _ _ := by
227	    simp_rw [comp_obj, F.comp_map, μ_natural_right_assoc, assoc, ← G.map_comp, μ_natural_right]
228	  associativity _ _ _ := by
229	    dsimp
230	    simp_rw [comp_whiskerRight, assoc, μ_natural_left_assoc, MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft_comp,
231	      assoc, μ_natural_right_assoc, ← associativity_assoc, ← G.map_comp, associativity]
232	
233	end
234	
235	end LaxMonoidal

237	/-- A functor `F : C ⥤ D` between monoidal categories is oplax monoidal if it is
238	equipped with morphisms `η : F.obj (𝟙_ C) ⟶ 𝟙 _D` and `δ X Y : F.obj (X ⊗ Y) ⟶ F.obj X ⊗ F.obj Y`,
239	satisfying the appropriate coherences. -/
240	@[ext]
241	class OplaxMonoidal (F : C ⥤ D) where
242	  /-- the counit morphism of a lax monoidal functor -/
243	  η (F) : F.obj (𝟙_ C) ⟶ 𝟙_ D
244	  /-- the cotensorator of an oplax monoidal functor -/
245	  δ (F) : ∀ X Y : C, F.obj (X ⊗ Y) ⟶ F.obj X ⊗ F.obj Y
246	  δ_natural_left (F) :
247	    ∀ {X Y : C} (f : X ⟶ Y) (X' : C),
248	      δ X X' ≫ F.map f ▷ F.obj X' = F.map (f ▷ X') ≫ δ Y X' := by
249	    cat_disch
250	  δ_natural_right (F) :
251	    ∀ {X Y : C} (X' : C) (f : X ⟶ Y),
252	      δ X' X ≫ F.obj X' ◁ F.map f = F.map (X' ◁ f) ≫ δ X' Y := by
253	    cat_disch
254	  /-- associativity of the tensorator -/
255	  oplax_associativity (F) :
256	    ∀ X Y Z : C,
257	      δ (X ⊗ Y) Z ≫ δ X Y ▷ F.obj Z ≫ (α_ (F.obj X) (F.obj Y) (F.obj Z)).hom =
258	        F.map (α_ X Y Z).hom ≫ δ X (Y ⊗ Z) ≫ F.obj X ◁ δ Y Z := by
259	    cat_disch
260	  -- unitality
261	  oplax_left_unitality (F) :
262	    ∀ X : C, (λ_ (F.obj X)).inv = F.map (λ_ X).inv ≫ δ (𝟙_ C) X ≫ η ▷ F.obj X := by
263	      cat_disch
264	  oplax_right_unitality (F) :
265	    ∀ X : C, (ρ_ (F.obj X)).inv = F.map (ρ_ X).inv ≫ δ X (𝟙_ C) ≫ F.obj X ◁ η := by
266	      cat_disch
267	
268	namespace OplaxMonoidal
269	
270	attribute [reassoc (attr := simp)] δ_natural_left δ_natural_right
271	
272	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
273	alias associativity := oplax_associativity
274	
275	@[simp, reassoc]
276	alias left_unitality := oplax_left_unitality
277	
278	@[simp, reassoc]
279	alias right_unitality := oplax_right_unitality
280	
281	section

283	variable (F : C ⥤ D) [F.OplaxMonoidal]

285	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
286	theorem δ_natural {X Y X' Y' : C} (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : X' ⟶ Y') :
287	    δ F X X' ≫ (F.map f ⊗ₘ F.map g) = F.map (f ⊗ₘ g) ≫ δ F Y Y' := by
288	  simp [tensorHom_def]
289	
290	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
291	theorem left_unitality_hom (X : C) :
292	    δ F (𝟙_ C) X ≫ η F ▷ F.obj X ≫ (λ_ (F.obj X)).hom = F.map (λ_ X).hom := by
293	  rw [← Category.assoc, ← Iso.eq_comp_inv, left_unitality, ← Category.assoc,
294	    ← F.map_comp, Iso.hom_inv_id, F.map_id, id_comp]
295	
296	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
297	theorem right_unitality_hom (X : C) :
298	    δ F X (𝟙_ C) ≫ F.obj X ◁ η F ≫ (ρ_ (F.obj X)).hom = F.map (ρ_ X).hom := by
299	  rw [← Category.assoc, ← Iso.eq_comp_inv, right_unitality, ← Category.assoc,
300	    ← F.map_comp, Iso.hom_inv_id, F.map_id, id_comp]
301

303	theorem associativity_inv (X Y Z : C) :

8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Generator

10	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Adjunction.PartialAdjoint

13	/-!
14	# Pullback of presheaves of modules
15	
16	Let `F : C ⥤ D` be a functor, `R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat` and `S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat` be presheaves
17	of rings, and `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` be a morphism of presheaves of rings,
18	we introduce the pullback functor `pullback : PresheafOfModules S ⥤ PresheafOfModules R`
19	as the left adjoint of `pushforward : PresheafOfModules R ⥤ PresheafOfModules S`.
20	The existence of this left adjoint functor is obtained under suitable universe assumptions.
21	
22	From the compatibility of `pushforward` with respect to composition, we deduce
23	similar pseudofunctor-like properties of the `pullback` functors.
24	
25	-/
26	
27	@[expose] public section
28	
29	universe v v₁ v₂ v₃ v₄ u₁ u₂ u₃ u₄ u
30	
31	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite Functor
32	
33	namespace PresheafOfModules
34	
35	section
36	
37	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
38	  {F : C ⥤ D} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)
39	  [(pushforward.{v} φ).IsRightAdjoint]
40	
41	/-- The pullback functor `PresheafOfModules S ⥤ PresheafOfModules R` induced by
42	a morphism of presheaves of rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, defined as the left adjoint
43	functor to the pushforward, when it exists. -/
44	noncomputable def pullback : PresheafOfModules.{v} S ⥤ PresheafOfModules.{v} R :=
45	  (pushforward.{v} φ).leftAdjoint
46	
47	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of rings `S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, this is the adjunction
48	between associated pullback and pushforward functors on the categories
49	of presheaves of modules. -/
50	noncomputable def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback.{v} φ ⊣ pushforward.{v} φ :=
51	  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (pushforward φ)
52	
53	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of rings `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, this is the property
54	that the (partial) left adjoint functor of `pushforward φ` is defined
55	on a certain object `M : PresheafOfModules S`. -/
56	abbrev pullbackObjIsDefined : ObjectProperty (PresheafOfModules.{v} S) :=
57	  (pushforward φ).leftAdjointObjIsDefined
58	
59	end
60	
61	section
62	
63	variable {C D : Type u} [SmallCategory C] [SmallCategory D]
64	  {F : C ⥤ D} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)
65	
66	/-- Given a morphism of presheaves of rings `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R`, where `F : C ⥤ D`,
67	`S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat`, `R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat` and `X : C`, the (partial) left adjoint
68	functor of `pushforward φ` is defined on the object `(free S).obj (yoneda.obj X)`:
69	this object shall be mapped to `(free R).obj (yoneda.obj (F.obj X))`. -/
70	noncomputable def pushforwardCompCoyonedaFreeYonedaCorepresentableBy (X : C) :
71	    (pushforward φ ⋙ coyoneda.obj (op ((free S).obj (yoneda.obj X)))).CorepresentableBy
72	      ((free R).obj (yoneda.obj (F.obj X))) where
73	  homEquiv {M} := freeYonedaEquiv.trans
74	    (freeYonedaEquiv (M := (pushforward φ).obj M)).symm
75	  homEquiv_comp {M N} g f := freeYonedaEquiv.injective (by
76	    dsimp
77	    erw [Equiv.apply_symm_apply, freeYonedaEquiv_comp]
78	    conv_rhs => erw [freeYonedaEquiv_comp]
79	    erw [Equiv.apply_symm_apply]
80	    rfl)
81	
82	lemma pullbackObjIsDefined_free_yoneda (X : C) :
83	    pullbackObjIsDefined φ ((free S).obj (yoneda.obj X)) :=
84	  (pushforwardCompCoyonedaFreeYonedaCorepresentableBy φ X).isCorepresentable
85	
86	lemma pullbackObjIsDefined_eq_top :
87	    pullbackObjIsDefined.{u} φ = ⊤ := by
88	  ext M
89	  simp only [Pi.top_apply, Prop.top_eq_true, iff_true]
90	  apply leftAdjointObjIsDefined_of_isColimit
91	    M.isColimitFreeYonedaCoproductsCokernelCofork
92	  rintro (_ | _)
93	  all_goals
94	    apply leftAdjointObjIsDefined_colimit _
95	      (fun _ ↦ pullbackObjIsDefined_free_yoneda _ _)
96	
97	instance : (pushforward.{u} φ).IsRightAdjoint :=
98	  isRightAdjoint_of_leftAdjointObjIsDefined_eq_top
99	    (pullbackObjIsDefined_eq_top φ)
100	
101	end
102	
103	section
104	
105	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D]
106	  {E : Type u₃} [Category.{v₃} E] {E' : Type u₄} [Category.{v₄} E']
107	
108	variable {F : C ⥤ D} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R)
109	  {G : D ⥤ E} {T : Eᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} (ψ : R ⟶ G.op ⋙ T)
110	
111	instance : (pushforward.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S)).IsRightAdjoint :=
112	  isRightAdjoint_of_iso (pushforwardId.{v} S).symm
113	
114	variable (S) in
115	/-- The pullback by the identity morphism identifies to the identity functor of the
116	category of presheaves of modules. -/
117	noncomputable def pullbackId : pullback.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
118	  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S))).leftAdjointIdIso (pushforwardId S)
119	
120	variable [(pushforward.{v} φ).IsRightAdjoint]
121	
122	section
123	
124	variable [(pushforward.{v} ψ).IsRightAdjoint]
125	
126	instance : (pushforward.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ whiskerLeft F.op ψ)).IsRightAdjoint :=
127	  isRightAdjoint_of_iso (pushforwardComp.{v} φ ψ)
128	
129	/-- The composition of two pullback functors on presheaves of modules identifies
130	to the pullback for the composition. -/
131	noncomputable def pullbackComp :
132	    pullback.{v} φ ⋙ pullback.{v} ψ ≅
133	      pullback.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ whiskerLeft F.op ψ) :=
134	  Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso
135	    (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{v} φ) (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{v} ψ)
136	    (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ whiskerLeft F.op ψ))
137	    (pushforwardComp φ ψ)
138	
139	variable {T' : E'ᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {G' : E ⥤ E'} (ψ' : T ⟶ G'.op ⋙ T')
140	  [(pushforward.{v} ψ').IsRightAdjoint]
141	
142	lemma pullback_assoc :
143	    isoWhiskerLeft _ (pullbackComp.{v} ψ ψ') ≪≫
144	      pullbackComp.{v} (G := G ⋙ G') φ (ψ ≫ whiskerLeft G.op ψ') =
145	    (associator _ _ _).symm ≪≫ isoWhiskerRight (pullbackComp.{v} φ ψ) _ ≪≫
146	        pullbackComp.{v} (F := F ⋙ G) (φ ≫ whiskerLeft F.op ψ) ψ' :=
147	  Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso_assoc _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ (pushforward_assoc φ ψ ψ')
148	
149	end
150	
151	lemma pullback_id_comp :
152	    pullbackComp.{v} (F := 𝟭 C) (𝟙 S) φ =
153	      isoWhiskerRight (pullbackId S) (pullback φ) ≪≫ Functor.leftUnitor _ :=
154	  Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso_id_comp _ _ _ _ (pushforward_comp_id φ)
155	
156	lemma pullback_comp_id :
157	    pullbackComp.{v} (G := 𝟭 _) φ (𝟙 R) =
158	      isoWhiskerLeft _ (pullbackId R) ≪≫ Functor.rightUnitor _ :=
159	  Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso_comp_id _ _ _ _ (pushforward_id_comp φ)
160	
161	end
162	
163	end PresheafOfModules

995	  leftAdjoint_μ X Y := by
996	    simp only [comp_obj, comp_μ, IsMonoidal.leftAdjoint_μ (adj := adj), id_obj,
997	      IsMonoidal.leftAdjoint_μ (adj := adj'), assoc, ← map_comp, comp_unit_app, comp_δ,
998	      comp_counit_app, ← tensorHom_comp_tensorHom, δ_natural_assoc, Functor.comp_map]
999	    simp

1001	end LaxMonoidal

1003	section OplaxMonoidal
1004	variable [G.LaxMonoidal]
1005	
1006	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
1007	/-- The left adjoint of a lax monoidal functor is oplax monoidal. -/
1008	@[simps -isSimp, implicit_reducible]
1009	def leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal : F.OplaxMonoidal where
1010	  η := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm (ε G)
1011	  δ X Y := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm ((adj.unit.app X ⊗ₘ adj.unit.app Y) ≫ μ G _ _)
1012	  δ_natural_left _ _ := by
1013	    rw [← Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right_symm,
1014	      ← Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left_symm, assoc, ← μ_natural_left]
1015	    simp [← tensorHom_id]
1016	  δ_natural_right _ _ := by
1017	    rw [← Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right_symm,
1018	      ← Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left_symm, assoc, ← μ_natural_right]
1019	    simp [← id_tensorHom]
1020	  oplax_associativity X Y Z := (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective (by
1021	    rw [← Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right_symm,
1022	      ← Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right_symm,
1023	      ← Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left_symm,
1024	      Equiv.apply_symm_apply, Equiv.apply_symm_apply, assoc, assoc]
1025	    conv_lhs =>
1026	      rw [homEquiv_counit, map_comp_assoc, map_comp,
1027	        ← μ_natural_left_assoc, map_comp, map_comp, tensorHom_def'_assoc]
1028	      dsimp
1029	      rw [← comp_whiskerRight_assoc]
1030	    conv_rhs =>
1031	      rw [← μ_natural_right, homEquiv_counit, map_comp_assoc,
1032	        map_comp, tensorHom_def_assoc, ← associator_naturality_left_assoc]
1033	      dsimp
1034	      rw [← MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft_comp_assoc, map_comp,
1035	        unit_naturality_assoc, MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft_comp,
1036	        unit_naturality_assoc, right_triangle_components, comp_id, assoc,
1037	        tensorHom_def, MonoidalCategory.whiskerLeft_comp_assoc,
1038	        ← associator_naturality_middle_assoc, ← associator_naturality_right_assoc,
1039	        ← associativity G, ← comp_whiskerRight_assoc, ← tensorHom_def,
1040	        ← whisker_exchange_assoc, ← comp_whiskerRight_assoc]
1041	    simp)
1042	  oplax_left_unitality _ := (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective (by
1043	    rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right,
1044	      Equiv.apply_symm_apply, assoc, ← μ_natural_left, ← tensorHom_id,
1045	      tensorHom_comp_tensorHom_assoc]
1046	    simp [tensorHom_def', homEquiv_unit, homEquiv_counit])
1047	  oplax_right_unitality _ := (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective (by
1048	    rw [Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left, Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right,
1049	      Equiv.apply_symm_apply, assoc, ← μ_natural_right, ← id_tensorHom,
1050	      tensorHom_comp_tensorHom_assoc]
1051	    simp [tensorHom_def, homEquiv_unit, homEquiv_counit])
1052	
1053	instance :
1054	    letI := adj.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal
1055	    adj.IsMonoidal := by
1056	  letI := adj.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal
1057	  refine ⟨?_, fun X Y ↦ ?_⟩
1058	  · simp [homEquiv_counit, leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal_η]
1059	  · simp [homEquiv_counit, ← μ_natural, leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal_δ]

open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory

input.new_string:
open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — `restrictScalars` is lax monoidal

The presheaf-of-modules restriction-of-scalars functor along a morphism of
presheaves of *commutative* rings is lax monoidal. Mathlib ships the sectionwise
fact `ModuleCat.restrictScalars f` is `LaxMonoidal`
(`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction`); here we lift it to
the presheaf level through the sectionwise presheaf monoidal structure
(`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal`). This is the sole project-side ingredient feeding
the oplax comparison `δ` of `pullback φ` (the mate of `pushforward φ`) used to
close `tensorObj_restrict_iso`. Per blueprint `lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal`. -/

namespace PresheafOfModules

universe v'

variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v'} C] {R S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- The lax-monoidal unit `ε` of `restrictScalars α`, assembled sectionwise from
`ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X)`'s lax-monoidal unit. -/
noncomputable def restrictScalarsLaxε
    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
    (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) ⟶
      (restrictScalars α).obj (𝟙_ (PresheafOfModules.{u} (S ⋙ forget₂ _ _))) where
  app X := Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom)
  naturality {X Y} f := sorry

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- The lax-monoidal tensorator `μ` of `restrictScalars α`, assembled sectionwise
from `ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X)`'s lax-monoidal tensorator. -/
noncomputable def restrictScalarsLaxμ
    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
    (M₁ M₂ : PresheafOfModules.{u} (S ⋙ forget₂ _ _)) :
    (restrictScalars α).obj M₁ ⊗ (restrictScalars α).obj M₂ ⟶
      (restrictScalars α).obj (M₁ ⊗ M₂) where
  app X := by
    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X)
  naturality {X Y} f := sorry

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- **`restrictScalars α` is lax monoidal** for a morphism `α` of presheaves of
commutative rings. Project-local lift of `ModuleCat.instLaxMonoidalRestrictScalars`. -/
noncomputable instance restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal
    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).LaxMonoidal where
  ε := restrictScalarsLaxε α
  μ M₁ M₂ := restrictScalarsLaxμ α M₁ M₂
  μ_natural_left := sorry
  μ_natural_right := sorry
  associativity := sorry
  left_unitality := sorry
  right_unitality := sorry

end PresheafOfModules

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

===file lines 250-310===
      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e'))

/-- **The substrate tensor of the structure sheaf with itself is the structure
sheaf.**

`tensorObj 𝒪_X 𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_X`, where `𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the
designated unit object. Built from the presheaf-level left unitor
`λ_ (𝟙_)` (the unit of the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal category is exactly
`SheafOfModules.unit.val`) under sheafification, composed with the
sheafification-adjunction counit isomorphism on the (already-sheaf) unit. -/
noncomputable def tensorObj_unit_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} :
    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
      (λ_ (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) ≪≫
    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app
      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)

/-- **Refining a trivialisation to a smaller open.** If `M` is trivialised on an
open `U` (`M.restrict U.ι ≅ 𝒪_U`), it is trivialised on every open `W ≤ U`.

The chart-chase is identical in spirit to `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`:
factor `W.ι = (X.homOfLE hWU) ≫ U.ι`, transport through `restrictFunctorCongr`
and `restrictFunctorComp` to identify `M.restrict W.ι` with
`(M.restrict U.ι).restrict (X.homOfLE hWU)`, restrict the given trivialisation
`e` along that open immersion, and identify the restricted unit with the unit
via `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` + `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (an isomorphism
because the open immersion's `Opens.map` is `Final`). -/
noncomputable def restrictIsoUnitOfLE {X : Scheme.{u}} {M : X.Modules} {U W : X.Opens}
    (hWU : W ≤ U)
    (e : M.restrict U.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U : Scheme).ringCatSheaf) :
    M.restrict W.ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (W : Scheme).ringCatSheaf := by
  have hWU' : W ≤ (𝟙 X) ⁻¹ᵁ U := hWU
  set j : (W : Scheme) ⟶ (U : Scheme) := Scheme.Hom.resLE (𝟙 X) U W hWU' with hj
  have hjι : j ≫ U.ι = W.ι := by rw [hj, Scheme.Hom.resLE_comp_ι, Category.comp_id]
  haveI : (TopologicalSpace.Opens.map j.base).Final :=
    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _
  -- M.restrict W.ι ≅ (pullback W.ι).obj M
  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback W.ι).app M ≪≫ ?_
  -- ≅ (pullback (j ≫ U.ι)).obj M
  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hjι.symm).app M ≪≫ ?_
  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)
  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp j U.ι).symm.app M ≪≫ ?_
  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj (M.restrict U.ι)
  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso
    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback U.ι).symm.app M) ≪≫ ?_
  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj 𝒪_U
  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullback j).mapIso e ≪≫ ?_
  -- ≅ 𝒪_W
  haveI hI : IsIso (SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit j.toRingCatSheafHom) := inferInstance
  exact @asIso _ _ _ _ _ hI

/-- **Substrate tensor commutes with restriction along an open immersion.**

`(tensorObj M N).restrict f ≅ tensorObj (M.restrict f) (N.restrict f)`.

input.code:
import Mathlib

open CategoryTheory MonoidalCategory Limits

namespace PresheafOfModules

universe v u
variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {R S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
noncomputable instance restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal
    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).LaxMonoidal where
  ε :=
    { app := fun X ↦ Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom)
      naturality := fun {X Y} f ↦ by
        ext r
        simp only [ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η]
        exact RingHom.congr_fun (congrArg RingCat.Hom.hom (α.naturality f)) r }
  μ M₁ M₂ :=
    { app := fun X ↦ by
        exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X)
      naturality := fun {X Y} f ↦
        ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun m₁ m₂ ↦ by
          simp; exact (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul f m₁ m₂).symm) }
  μ_natural_left := by
    intro X Y f X'; ext1 Z
    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ_natural_left (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
      (f.app Z) (X'.obj Z)
  μ_natural_right := by
    intro X Y X' f; ext1 Z
    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ_natural_right (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
      (X'.obj Z) (f.app Z)
  associativity := by
    intro M N P; ext1 Z
    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.associativity (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
      (M.obj Z) (N.obj Z) (P.obj Z)
  left_unitality := by
    intro M; ext1 Z
    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.left_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom) (M.obj Z)
  right_unitality := by
    intro M; ext1 Z
    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.right_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom) (M.obj Z)

-- Next: pushforward lax
variable {D : Type u} [Category.{v} D] {F : C ⥤ D}
  {Rc : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}} {Sc : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}

example (φ : Sc ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ (F.op ⋙ Rc) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
    (pushforward φ).LaxMonoidal :=
  inferInstanceAs ((pushforward₀ F (Rc ⋙ forget₂ _ _) ⋙ restrictScalars φ).LaxMonoidal)

end PresheafOfModules

input.old_string:
right_unitality := by
    intro M
    ext1 Z
    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.right_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
      (M.obj Z)

end PresheafOfModules
input.new_string:
right_unitality := by
    intro M
    ext1 Z
    exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.right_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom)
      (M.obj Z)

section Pushforward

variable {D : Type u} [Category.{v'} D] {F : C ⥤ D}
  {Rc : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}} {Sc : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
/-- **`pushforward φ` is lax monoidal** for a morphism `φ` of presheaves of
commutative rings: it is the composite of the monoidal `pushforward₀OfCommRingCat`
with the lax-monoidal `restrictScalars φ` (`restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`), so the
composite is lax monoidal by `Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp`. Project-local; supplies
the hypothesis of `Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal` for `pullback φ`. -/
noncomputable instance pushforwardLaxMonoidal
    (φ : Sc ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ (F.op ⋙ Rc) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
    (pushforward φ).LaxMonoidal :=
  inferInstanceAs ((pushforward₀ F (Rc ⋙ forget₂ _ _) ⋙ restrictScalars φ).LaxMonoidal)

end Pushforward

end PresheafOfModules

/-- **The substrate tensor of the structure sheaf with itself is the structure
sheaf.**

`tensorObj 𝒪_X 𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_X`, where `𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the
designated unit object. Built from the presheaf-level left unitor
`λ_ (𝟙_)` (the unit of the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal category is exactly
`SheafOfModules.unit.val`) under sheafification, composed with the
sheafification-adjunction counit isomorphism on the (already-sheaf) unit. -/
noncomputable def tensorObj_unit_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} :
    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
      (λ_ (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) ≪≫
    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app
      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)

/-- **Refining a trivialisation to a smaller open.** If `M` is trivialised on an
open `U` (`M.restrict U.ι ≅ 𝒪_U`), it is trivialised on every open `W ≤ U`.

The chart-chase is identical in spirit to `LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial.pullback`:

because the open immersion's `Opens.map` is `Final`). -/

import Mathlib
open CategoryTheory MonoidalCategory Limits AlgebraicGeometry

namespace PresheafOfModules
universe v u
variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {R S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
noncomputable instance restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal
    (α : R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ S ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).LaxMonoidal where
  ε := { app := fun X ↦ Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom)
         naturality := fun {X Y} f ↦ by
           ext r; simp only [ModuleCat.restrictScalars_η]
           exact RingHom.congr_fun (congrArg RingCat.Hom.hom (α.naturality f)) r }
  μ M₁ M₂ := { app := fun X ↦ by
                 exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ (ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app X).hom) (M₁.obj X) (M₂.obj X)
               naturality := fun {X Y} f ↦ ModuleCat.MonoidalCategory.tensor_ext (fun m₁ m₂ ↦ by
                 simp; exact (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj_map_tmul f m₁ m₂).symm) }
  μ_natural_left := by intro X Y f X'; ext1 Z
                       exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ_natural_left (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom) (f.app Z) (X'.obj Z)
  μ_natural_right := by intro X Y X' f; ext1 Z
                        exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.μ_natural_right (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom) (X'.obj Z) (f.app Z)
  associativity := by intro M N P; ext1 Z
                      exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.associativity (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom) (M.obj Z) (N.obj Z) (P.obj Z)
  left_unitality := by intro M; ext1 Z
                       exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.left_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom) (M.obj Z)
  right_unitality := by intro M; ext1 Z
                        exact Functor.LaxMonoidal.right_unitality (F := ModuleCat.restrictScalars (α.app Z).hom) (M.obj Z)
section Pushforward
variable {D : Type u} [Category.{v} D] {F : C ⥤ D}
  {Rc : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}} {Sc : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}}
set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
noncomputable instance pushforwardLaxMonoidal
    (φ : Sc ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat ⟶ (F.op ⋙ Rc) ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat) :
    (pushforward φ).LaxMonoidal :=
  inferInstanceAs ((pushforward₀ F (Rc ⋙ forget₂ _ _) ⋙ restrictScalars φ).LaxMonoidal)
end Pushforward
end PresheafOfModules

-- THE TEST: does the project's φ get the LaxMonoidal instance?
example {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : Y ⟶ X) [IsOpenImmersion f] :
    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom.hom).LaxMonoidal := inferInstance

17	/-!
18	# The category of sheaves of modules over a scheme
19	
20	In this file, we define the abelian category of sheaves of modules
21	`X.Modules` over a scheme `X`, and study its basic functoriality.
22	
23	-/
24	
25	@[expose] public section
26	
27	universe t u

32
